name: FIAV (International Federation of Vexillological Associations)

colors.symbolism: the field — Pantone Matching System U293
the halyards — Pantone Matching System U123

description: A blue field charged with two interlaced yellow halyards forming a sheet-bend knot, extending horizontally from hoist to fly.
history: Designed by Klaes Sierksma and slightly modified by the organising committee of the Second International Congress of Vexillology, the flag was introduced on 3 September 1967 at Rüschlikon, Switzerland, when FIAV itself was provisionally organised. FIAV was officially created on 7 September 1969 at the Third ICV in Boston. The central knot device is reused on FIAV's Laureate and Fellow of the Federation medals.

features.role: two interlaced halyards forming a sheet-bend knot
features.symbolism: the bond among vexillological associations worldwide
